summary: Claude Chrome Extension Access Check Failed

message.content: Organize as tarefas no projeto da raiz (esta pasta)

content.thinking:
O usuário está pedindo para organizar as tarefas no projeto. Preciso primeiro entender o estado atual do projeto lendo os arquivos de estado do harness:

1. `claude-progress.txt` - histórico de sessões
2. `features.json` - features com status

Vou ler esses arquivos para entender o que precisa ser feito.

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.1",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-17",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "parallelExecution": {
    "description": "Parallel execution configuration for git worktree-based concurrent development",
    "runner": "bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts",
    "worktreeDir": ".trees/",
    "estimatedSpeedup": "2.5x",
    "waves": [
      {
        "wave": 1,
        "description": "Initial parallel - isolated features + grouped modifications",
        "maxConcurrency": 3,
        "groups": [
          {"id": "group-A", "features": ["F023"], "sequential": false, "files": ["MicroLayer.tsx"]},
          {"id": "group-B", "features": ["F123", "F025"], "sequential": true, "files": ["App.tsx"]},
          {"id": "group-C", "features": ["F016", "F017"], "sequential": true, "files": ["InfiniteCanvas.tsx"]}
        ]
      },
      {
        "wave": 2,
        "description": "Keyboard features + Task panel (after Wave 1 merge)",
        "maxConcurrency": 2,
        "dependsOn": 1,
        "groups": [
          {"id": "group-D", "features": ["F027", "F028"], "sequential": true, "files": ["InfiniteCanvas.tsx"]},
          {"id": "group-E", "features": ["F026"], "sequential": false, "files": ["App.tsx"]}
        ]
      },
      {
        "wave": 3,
        "description": "Keyboard task navigation",
        "maxConcurrency": 1,
        "dependsOn": 2,
        "groups": [
          {"id": "group-F", "features": ["F021"], "sequential": false, "files": ["InfiniteCanvas.tsx", "layers"]}
        ]
      },
      {
        "wave": 4,
        "description": "Final accessibility (depends on F021)",
        "maxConcurrency": 1,
        "dependsOn": 3,
        "groups": [
          {"id": "group-G", "features": ["F022"], "sequential": false, "files": ["InfiniteCanvas.tsx", "MicroLayer.tsx"]}
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "URGENT-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Auditar e documentar uso de React hooks - migrar apenas estados críticos para external stores",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE (REAVALIADO) - Audit done: cameraPosRef workaround resolves critical stale closure bug (F122). Other states (isInitialized, canvasSize, isPanning, panStart) are UI-only or one-time - no issues. useRef for momentum/animation is correct pattern. Added React hooks vs external stores guidelines to CLAUDE.md. Full migration to external stores would be over-engineering. If new stale closure bugs appear, migrate case by case. 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "PERF-001",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Adicionar ferramenta de desenvolvimento para métricas de performance WebGL em tempo real + logging detalhado",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented r3f-perf 7.2.3 with PerfMonitor component. Features: visual panel (FPS/MS/MB/GPU/draw calls/triangles), logging system with circular buffer (60s), session recording API (START_SESSION/STOP_SESSION). Toggle via 'P' key or navbar button. Global API: window.__PERF_LOG__, __PERF_EXPORT__, __PERF_START_SESSION__, __PERF_STOP_SESSION__, __PERF_CLEAR__. Known issue: r3f-perf has expandShaderIncludes console spam due to troika-three-utils compatibility with Three.js 0.170+ (tracked in r3f-perf#58). Does not affect functionality. Documented in docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-003",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Atualizar protocolo de testes visuais do Harness para incluir métricas de performance (visual + logging)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "PERF-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Updated docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md with comprehensive performance testing section. Added: Quick Check (visual) and Detailed Check (logging) approaches, Playwright integration examples, approval criteria matrix, when to use each approach guide, known issues section. Updated testingProtocol in features.json with performance criteria and mandatory step 5. Performance testing is now MANDATORY for all features. 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-004",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Migrar tarefas POC do archive para fluxo normal de features pendentes",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Migrated 12 POC features (F016-F023, F025-F028) from archive to active features list. Removed -POC suffix, assigned priorities (2-4 based on importance), set proper blockedBy dependencies. F024-POC archived (covered by F116). Archive now only contains F117 and F024-POC. Added migrationNote to archive section. 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Configure Playwright MCP for headless without focus stealing",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Playwright MCP configured with --headless flag in .mcp.json. Deny permissions added to block access to personal directories."
    },
    {
      "id": "QA-PLAN-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking (planning mode)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md with WebGL testing protocol. Updated session-start.sh and end-of-turn-check.sh hooks. Protocol includes: typecheck, visual test, console check, interaction test, performance check sequence."
    },
    {
      "id": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "category": "research",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with comprehensive research on: Figma/Google Maps/iOS patterns, mouse/touch/pointer events, wheel normalization (Facebook pattern), momentum physics, cross-platform scroll differences, accessibility requirements. Includes implementation recommendations for F118-F121."
    },
    {
      "id": "F118",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Momentum/inertia panning (velocity-based drag)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented with velocity tracking during drag, friction-based animation loop (0.93 friction), and momentum stops at velocity < 0.5. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F119",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Smooth zoom UX (iOS/Google Maps feel)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented quadOut easing for zoom animations (250ms), wheel event accumulation for trackpad smoothing, pinch-to-zoom support via Pointer Events API, animated zoom for +/- buttons and Reset. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F120",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Double-click to zoom in (BUG FIX)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented with 350ms cubicOut animation. Zooms 2x toward click point, respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x). Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F121",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Cross-platform scroll/input sensitivity normalization",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented deltaMode normalization (LINE/PAGE to pixels), trackpad vs mouse detection by event frequency, adaptive sensitivity (mouse: 0.003, trackpad: 0.001), zoom delta clamping (±0.5 per event). Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F122",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "BUG FIX: Elastic/jump effect when dragging canvas",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Root cause: React state batching caused stale cameraPos when starting new drag during momentum. Fix: Added cameraPosRef for synchronous position tracking. handlePointerDown now uses ref instead of state. All setCameraPos calls sync to ref via updateCameraPos(). Tested with rapid drag interruptions via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F116",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 3,
      "description": "Performance optimization (60fps)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F110",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Optimized MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from 50 individual meshes to 1 InstancedMesh. All layers now use InstancedMesh for optimal draw calls. MicroLayer already had frustum culling and optimized rendering. Visual tests pass at all LOD levels (MACRO/MESO/MICRO). No console errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "F123",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 3,
      "description": "Minimap navigation (bird's eye view)",
      "steps": [
        "Research minimap patterns in design tools (Figma, Canva) and games",
        "Create minimap component showing entire canvas at small scale",
        "Show viewport rectangle indicating current view position",
        "Allow click/drag on minimap to navigate",
        "Position in corner (bottom-right recommended)",
        "Add toggle to show/hide minimap"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "F110",
      "notes": "FEATURE REQUEST: Add minimap like games/design tools to help users locate themselves in the larger canvas. Shows entire 10-year timeline + 50 trains at small scale with viewport indicator."
    },
    {
      "id": "TECH-DEBT-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Migrar camera state para @mms/toolkit state-machine (substituir React hooks)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Migrated all viewport state from React useState to @mms/toolkit external store. Created viewport slice (apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts) with camera, canvasSize, momentum, animation, pan states. Created canvas machine (canvas-machine.ts) with useCanvasSelector, canvasActions, canvasStore exports. Migrated InfiniteCanvas.tsx to use store.getState() in animation loops (solves stale closure). Removed cameraPosRef workaround (no longer needed). All LOD layers (MACRO, MESO, MICRO), zoom buttons, and navigation verified via Playwright MCP. 2025-12-17."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-002",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Reduce harness file sizes (archive completed features)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - features.json: 998 → 674 lines (-32%) by removing steps from completed features. claude-progress.txt: 1790 → 547 lines (-69%) by summarizing historical sessions. Total reduction: ~55%. Backup files created (features-full-backup.json, claude-progress-full-backup.txt). 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F124",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Sidebar and Timeline interactive navigation",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented clickable train IDs in sidebar and clickable date labels in timeline. Navigation uses smooth 350ms animation. Hover effects with background highlight. Tooltips show 'Click to navigate to...'. Search/filter and keyboard shortcuts deferred to future enhancement. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Canvas renders and initializes without errors",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "POC validated semantic zoom concept. Production app: apps/canvas"
    },
    {

1→# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

     3→## Session: 2025-12-17 (F017 - Ctrl+Scroll Horizontal Pan) - COMPLETE ✅
     4→
     5→### F017: Ctrl+Scroll for horizontal pan - COMPLETE
     6→
     7→**Objective:**
     8→Enable horizontal pan navigation using Ctrl+Scroll, converting vertical scroll (deltaY) to horizontal camera movement.
     9→
    10→**Implementation:**
    11→
    12→1. **Added SCROLL_PAN_SENSITIVITY constant (1.5):**
    13→   - Located at InfiniteCanvas.tsx line ~273
    14→   - Converts wheel delta to world units for smooth panning
    15→
    16→2. **Modified handleWheel function:**
    17→   - Added Ctrl key detection via `e.ctrlKey`
    18→   - When Ctrl is held, converts deltaY to horizontal pan instead of zoom
    19→   - Uses existing deltaMode normalization for cross-platform support
    20→   - Divides by zoom for consistent world-space movement at any zoom level
    21→   - Calls existing `updateCameraPos()` and `notifyViewportChange()`
    22→
    23→3. **Integration with existing systems:**
    24→   - Respects world bounds via `clampCameraToWorld()`
    25→   - Works alongside existing zoom (no modifier) and F016 (Shift+scroll for vertical pan)
    26→   - Stops momentum on any wheel interaction
    27→

    40→- Implementation verified via TypeScript compilation and code review

   104→## Session: 2025-12-17 (Parallel Harness Setup) - COMPLETE ✅
   105→
   106→### HARNESS-005: Parallel Execution Infrastructure - COMPLETE
   107→
   108→**Objective:**
   109→Create infrastructure to run multiple features in parallel using git worktrees, enabling ~2.5x faster development.
   110→
   111→**Research:**
   112→- Studied [Anthropic's Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)

   115→
   116→**Analysis of Pending Features (10 total):**
   117→
   118→```
   119→WAVE 1 (Can run in parallel - 3 groups):
   120→├── Group A: F023 (Dependency lines) → MicroLayer.tsx [LOW risk]
   121→├── Group B: F123, F025 (Minimap) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk, sequential]
   122→└── Group C: F016, F017 (Scroll modifiers) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]

   126→└── Group E: F026 (Task details panel) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk]

   130→
   131→WAVE 4 (After Wave 3 merge - 1 group):
   132→└── Group G: F022 (ARIA labels) → Multiple files [LOW risk]
   133→```
   134→
   135→**Files Created:**
   136→- `scripts/parallel-config.ts` - Wave/group configuration with conflict analysis
   137→- `scripts/parallel-runner.ts` - Interactive parallel execution runner
   138→- `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` - Complete documentation
   139→
   140→**Files Modified:**
   141→- `.gitignore` - Added `.trees/` for worktree directories
   142→- `features.json` - Added `parallelExecution` section with waves configuration
   143→
   144→**Key Features of Parallel Runner:**
   145→1. **Git worktree management** - Creates isolated working directories
   146→2. **Terminal session spawning** - Opens separate Claude sessions per feature
   147→3. **Isolation prompts** - Each session restricted to its feature scope
   148→4. **Wave dependencies** - Prevents starting Wave N before Wave N-1 completes
   149→5. **Merge helper** - Automates feature branch merging
   150→6. **Status display** - Shows progress across all waves/groups
   151→7. **macOS notifications** - Alerts for session events
   152→8. **Cleanup command** - Removes all worktrees
   153→
   154→**Speedup Calculation:**
   155→- Sequential: 10 features = 10 sessions
   156→- Parallel: 4 waves (2+2+1+1) = ~4 "effective sessions"
   157→- Estimated speedup: **2.5x**
   158→
   159→**Usage:**
   160→```bash
   161→# Show status
   162→bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts status
   163→
   164→# Execute Wave 1
   165→bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts wave 1
   166→
   167→# Merge completed feature
   168→bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts merge F023
   169→```

   322→
   323→### TECH-DEBT-001: Migrate Camera State to @mms/toolkit State Machine - COMPLETE
   324→
   325→**Objective:**
   326→Replace React useState/useRef with @mms/toolkit external store to solve stale closure problems in animation loops.
   327→
   328→**Implementation:**
   329→
   330→1. **Created viewport slice (`apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts`):**
   331→   - ViewportSliceState interface with camera, canvasSize, momentum, animation, pan states
   332→   - createSyncSlice with all reducers: setCamera, setMomentum, startAnimation, startPan, etc.
   333→   - Cache policy: 'session' (resets when app closes)
   334→
   335→2. **Created canvas machine (`apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts`):**
   336→   - createMachine with viewport slice
   337→   - Exports: useCanvas, useCanvasSelector, canvasActions, canvasStore
   338→   - Context with userID: null (anonymous canvas)
   339→
   340→3. **Migrated InfiniteCanvas.tsx:**
   341→   - Replaced useState calls with useCanvasSelector for reactive state
   342→   - Animation loops use canvasStore.getState() for synchronous access (no stale closures)
   343→   - Pan/zoom handlers use canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.xxx())
   344→   - Removed cameraPosRef workaround (no longer needed with external store)
   345→
   346→**Errors Fixed:**
   347→- `cache: { strategy: 'none' }` → `cache: { policy: 'session' }` (toolkit requires 'policy' field)
   348→- `context: {}` → `context: { userID: null }` (toolkit requires userID in context)
   349→

   530→### PERF-001: Performance Monitor with Logging System - COMPLETE
   531→
   532→**Objective:**
   533→Add real-time WebGL performance metrics tool with detailed logging for both visual and programmatic analysis.
   534→
   535→**Implementation:**
   536→
   537→1. **r3f-perf Integration:**
   538→   - Installed r3f-perf 7.2.3 via `bun add r3f-perf`
   539→   - Visual panel shows: GPU, CPU, FPS, draw calls, triangles, geometries, textures, shaders
   540→
   541→2. **PerfMonitor Component (`apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx`):**
   542→   - ~400 lines of code with types, logging system, and visual panel
   543→   - PerfLogger sub-component tracks metrics every frame
   544→   - Circular buffer stores last 60s of performance data
   545→   - Rolling averages calculated over 60-frame windows
   546→
   547→3. **Global Console API:**
   548→   - `window.__PERF_LOG__` - Access log buffer (last 60s)
   549→   - `window.__PERF_EXPORT__()` - Export full log as array
   550→   - `window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name)` - Start recording session
   551→   - `window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()` - Stop and get aggregated stats
   552→   - `window.__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__()` - Check active session
   553→   - `window.__PERF_CLEAR__()` - Clear log buffer
   554→
   555→4. **Session Statistics:**
   556→   - FPS: avg, min, max, p1 (worst 1%), p99 (best 99%)
   557→   - MS: avg, max frame time
   558→   - Frame counts: below 30fps, below 60fps
   559→   - Duration and total frames
   560→
   561→5. **UI Integration:**
   562→   - Toggle visibility via 'P' key
   563→   - "Perf" button in navbar
   564→   - Panel positioned top-right
   565→   - Runs in development mode by default
   566→
   567→**Known Issue:**
   568→- r3f-perf causes console spam with `expandShaderIncludes TypeError`
   569→- Root cause: troika-three-utils 0.49.0 incompatible with Three.js 0.170+
   570→- Fix exists in troika-three-utils 0.50.3+ but r3f-perf hasn't updated
   571→- Tracked in: https://github.com/utsuboco/r3f-perf/issues/58
   572→- Does NOT affect functionality - panel and logging work correctly

   711→### F116: Performance optimization (60fps) - COMPLETE
   712→
   713→**Objective:**
   714→Ensure 60fps performance during pan/zoom with InstancedMesh optimizations and frustum culling.
   715→
   716→**Analysis:**
   717→Reviewed all three LOD layers to identify performance bottlenecks:
   718→
   719→1. **MesoLayer (BEFORE):**
   720→   - TaskBlocks: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   721→   - ConflictGlows: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   722→   - RowBackgrounds: **50 individual meshes = 50 draw calls** ❌
   723→

   765→
   766→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅
   767→
   768→### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE
   769→
   770→**Problem:**
   771→Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.
   772→
   773→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   774→React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
   775→1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
   776→2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
   777→3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back
   778→
   779→**Solution Implemented:**
   780→1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
   781→2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
   782→3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   783→   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   784→   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   785→   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   786→   - `handlePinchZoom`
   787→   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   788→   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
   789→4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state
   790→
   791→**Files Changed:**
   792→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
   793→- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)
   794→

   797→- ✅ Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect
   798→- ✅ Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning
   799→- ✅ No console errors

   819→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F120 + F121 - Double-click zoom + Scroll normalization) - COMPLETE ✅
   820→
   821→### F120: Double-click to zoom in - COMPLETE
   822→
   823→**Objective:**
   824→Implement double-click to zoom in 2x centered on click location.
   825→
   826→**Implementation:**
   827→- Already implemented in F119 (smooth zoom) session
   828→- `handleDoubleClick` function in InfiniteCanvas.tsx (lines 1009-1074)
   829→- Zooms 2x toward click point with 350ms cubicOut animation
   830→- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit
   831→
   832→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
   833→- ✅ Double-click at 1.00x → 2.00x (2x zoom)
   834→- ✅ Double-click at 2.00x → 4.00x (2x zoom)
   835→- ✅ Double-click at 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)
   836→- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)
   837→- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing
   838→- ✅ No console errors
   839→
   840→### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE
   841→
   842→**Objective:**
   843→Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.
   844→
   845→**Implementation:**
   846→
   847→1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
   848→   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
   849→   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
   850→   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly
   851→
   852→2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
   853→   - Track event frequency with timestamps
   854→   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
   855→   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
   856→   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode
   857→
   858→3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
   859→   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
   860→   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)
   861→
   862→4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
   863→   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
   864→   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events
   865→
   866→**Constants Added:**
   867→```typescript
   868→LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
   869→PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
   870→MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
   871→TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
   872→MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
   873→TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
   874→TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
   875→```
   876→
   877→**Files Changed:**
   878→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)
   879→- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: true)

   883→- ✅ Scroll up (deltaY=-500) zooms in: 0.61x → 1.00x
   884→- ✅ No console errors
   885→- ✅ Smooth zoom animation
   886→
   887→**Next:** F116 (Performance), F117 (Visual parity)
   888→
   889→---
   890→
   891→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅
   892→
   893→### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE
   894→
   895→**Objective:**
   896→Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.
   897→
   898→**Implementation:**
   899→
   900→1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   901→   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   902→   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   903→   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity
   904→
   905→2. **Momentum Animation:**
   906→   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   907→   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   908→   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   909→   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units
   910→
   911→3. **Integration:**
   912→   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   913→   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   914→   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation
   915→
   916→**Constants Used:**
   917→```typescript
   918→MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
   919→MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
   920→MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
   921→```
   922→
   923→**Files Changed:**
   924→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
   925→- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)
   926→
   927→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
   928→- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)

   930→- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
   931→- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
   932→- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum
   933→

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-17 (F017 - Ctrl+Scroll Horizontal Pan) - COMPLETE ✅

### F017: Ctrl+Scroll for horizontal pan - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Enable horizontal pan navigation using Ctrl+Scroll, converting vertical scroll (deltaY) to horizontal camera movement.

**Implementation:**

1. **Added SCROLL_PAN_SENSITIVITY constant (1.5):**
   - Located at InfiniteCanvas.tsx line ~273
   - Converts wheel delta to world units for smooth panning

2. **Modified handleWheel function:**
   - Added Ctrl key detection via `e.ctrlKey`
   - When Ctrl is held, converts deltaY to horizontal pan instead of zoom
   - Uses existing deltaMode normalization for cross-platform support
   - Divides by zoom for consistent world-space movement at any zoom level
   - Calls existing `updateCameraPos()` and `notifyViewportChange()`

3. **Integration with existing systems:**
   - Respects world bounds via `clampCameraToWorld()`
   - Works alongside existing zoom (no modifier) and F016 (Shift+scroll for vertical pan)
   - Stops momentum on any wheel interaction

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (SCROLL_PAN_SENSITIVITY constant, handleWheel Ctrl check)
- `features.json` (F017 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App loads correctly with all layers
- ✅ Sidebar and timeline render properly
- ✅ Console shows only pre-existing warnings (expandShaderIncludes from r3f-perf)
- ✅ Code review confirms correct Ctrl+Scroll implementation

**Technical Notes:**
- Playwright synthetic WheelEvents have limitations with modifier key detection on WebGL canvas
- Implementation verified via TypeScript compilation and code review
- Pattern follows existing F016 (Shift+Scroll) structure

**Parallel Execution Context:**
- Working in git worktree `.trees/f017`
- Part of Wave 1, Group C (sequential with F016)
- Feature branch: `feat/f017`

---

## Session: 2025-12-17 (F025 Minimap - Parallel Worktree) - COMPLETE ✅

### F025: Time Scrubber Minimap - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Create a bird's eye view minimap showing the entire canvas at small scale, allowing quick navigation.

**Worktree Context:**
- Branch: `feat/f025`
- Worktree: `.trees/f025/`
- Part of Wave 1, Group B (sequential with F123)

**Implementation:**

**Files Created:**
- `apps/canvas/src/components/Minimap.tsx` (410 lines)
  - Heatmap visualization (40x10 grid cells)
  - Viewport rectangle overlay showing current view
  - Click/drag navigation to world coordinates
  - Toggle visibility button
  - BlueprintJS Card with SVG rendering
  - Color interpolation (amber-100 to red-600)

**Files Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx`
  - Added Minimap import and component
  - Added minimapVisible state
  - Added camera position selectors from canvas state
  - Added handleMinimapNavigate and handleMinimapToggle handlers
  - Added TOTAL_DAYS constant

**Key Implementation Details:**
- Minimap dimensions: 200x80 pixels
- Grid: 40 columns (days) × 10 rows (trains)
- World bounds: 36500px × 1200px (10 years × 50 trains)
- Coordinate conversion: worldToMinimap/minimapToWorld functions

- Viewport rectangle updates with camera position changes
- Click navigation converts minimap coords to day/train index

**Visual Testing (Playwright MCP):**

- ✅ Heatmap visualization with color gradient
- ✅ Viewport rectangle visible and positioned correctly
- ✅ Click navigation works (tested: Jan 2024 → Dec 2028)
- ✅ Toggle visibility works (hide → show button → restore)
- ✅ Scale indicator shows "10 years × 50 trains"

**Console Errors:**

- No new errors from F025 implementation

---

## Session: 2025-12-17 (Parallel Harness Setup) - COMPLETE ✅

### HARNESS-005: Parallel Execution Infrastructure - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Create infrastructure to run multiple features in parallel using git worktrees, enabling ~2.5x faster development.

**Research:**
- Studied [Anthropic's Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- Researched git worktree patterns for AI agent parallel development
- Analyzed file conflict potential for all 10 pending features

**Analysis of Pending Features (10 total):**

```
WAVE 1 (Can run in parallel - 3 groups):
├── Group A: F023 (Dependency lines) → MicroLayer.tsx [LOW risk]
├── Group B: F123, F025 (Minimap) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk, sequential]
└── Group C: F016, F017 (Scroll modifiers) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]

WAVE 2 (After Wave 1 merge - 2 groups):
├── Group D: F027, F028 (Keyboard shortcuts) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]
└── Group E: F026 (Task details panel) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk]

WAVE 3 (After Wave 2 merge - 1 group):
└── Group F: F021 (Keyboard task navigation) → Multiple files [HIGH risk]

WAVE 4 (After Wave 3 merge - 1 group):
└── Group G: F022 (ARIA labels) → Multiple files [LOW risk]
```

**Files Created:**
- `scripts/parallel-config.ts` - Wave/group configuration with conflict analysis
- `scripts/parallel-runner.ts` - Interactive parallel execution runner
- `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` - Complete documentation

**Files Modified:**
- `.gitignore` - Added `.trees/` for worktree directories
- `features.json` - Added `parallelExecution` section with waves configuration

**Key Features of Parallel Runner:**
1. **Git worktree management** - Creates isolated working directories
2. **Terminal session spawning** - Opens separate Claude sessions per feature
3. **Isolation prompts** - Each session restricted to its feature scope
4. **Wave dependencies** - Prevents starting Wave N before Wave N-1 completes
5. **Merge helper** - Automates feature branch merging
6. **Status display** - Shows progress across all waves/groups
7. **macOS notifications** - Alerts for session events
8. **Cleanup command** - Removes all worktrees

**Speedup Calculation:**
- Sequential: 10 features = 10 sessions
- Parallel: 4 waves (2+2+1+1) = ~4 "effective sessions"
- Estimated speedup: **2.5x**

**Usage:**
```bash
# Show status
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts status

# Execute Wave 1
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts wave 1

# Merge completed feature
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts merge F023
```

---

## Session: 2025-12-17 (F020 Tooltip) - COMPLETE ✅

### F020: Tooltip shows task details on hover - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Show task details in a tooltip when hovering over tasks in the canvas.

**Implementation:**

1. **Created TaskTooltip component (`apps/canvas/src/components/TaskTooltip.tsx`):**
   - Receives `taskId`, `mouseX`, `mouseY` props
   - Fetches task data from generator using `getData()`
   - Shows: task type with icon, date range, train ID, duration, conflict status
   - BlueprintJS Tags and Icons for consistent styling
   - Dark tooltip style matching BlueprintJS design

2. **Tooltip positioning:**
   - Positioned 16px offset from cursor (bottom-right by default)
   - Viewport boundary detection - flips to left/top if near edges
   - `pointerEvents: 'none'` to avoid interfering with canvas interactions

3. **Task details displayed:**
   - Type icon: wrench (maintenance), build (overhaul), eye-open (inspection)
   - Type tag with Intent color (PRIMARY, WARNING, NONE)
   - Conflict tag (red, with warning icon) when hasConflict=true
   - Train ID (monospace)
   - Date range (formatted: "Mar 15, 2024")
   - Duration in days

4. **Integration in App.tsx:**
   - Added mouse position tracking via onMouseMove on canvas container
   - Connected hoveredTaskId from interaction state slice via useCanvasSelector
   - TaskTooltip rendered outside canvas for proper z-index

**Files Created:**
- `apps/canvas/src/components/TaskTooltip.tsx` (~180 lines)

**Files Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (imports, mouse tracking, TaskTooltip component)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Tooltip appears on task hover
- ✅ Shows task type (Maintenance) with icon

- ✅ Shows "Conflict" tag on conflict tasks (Overhaul with conflict)

## Session: 2025-12-17 (F019 Click to Select) - COMPLETE ✅

### F019: Click to select task - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement click-to-select functionality for task blocks with visual feedback.

**Implementation:**

1. **Pan/click discrimination in InfiniteCanvas.tsx:**
   - Added `pointerMovedRef` and `pointerDownPosRef` refs to track pointer movement
   - Uses 8px threshold to distinguish click from pan gesture
   - Movement greater than 8px is treated as pan, less is a click

2. **handleClick callback:**
   - Only processes clicks if `pointerMovedRef.current === false`
   - Uses existing `findTaskAtPosition()` for hit testing
   - Toggle selection: clicking same task deselects it
   - Background click: clears current selection
   - Dispatches to existing interaction state slice

3. **MesoLayer selection highlight:**
   - Added `selectedTaskId` prop to TaskBlocks component
   - Selection color: cyan (#06b6d4) - distinct from hover brightness
   - Higher z-offset (0.15) than hover (0.05) for visual stacking
   - Selection takes precedence over hover styling

4. **MicroLayer selection highlight:**
   - Added `selectedTaskId` prop to TaskCard
   - Uses same cyan selection color as MesoLayer
   - Ring border style for consistency

**Technical Notes:**
- Selection state already existed in interaction slice (prepared in F018)
- Pan gesture detection prevents accidental selections during navigation
- Selection persists across zoom/pan operations
- Clearing hover during pan prevents stale hover highlights

**Files Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (click handling, pan/click discrimination)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx` (selection color, z-ordering)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx` (selection prop, styling)

## Session: 2025-12-17 (TECH-DEBT-001 Implementation) - COMPLETE ✅

### TECH-DEBT-001: Migrate Camera State to @mms/toolkit State Machine - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Replace React useState/useRef with @mms/toolkit external store to solve stale closure problems in animation loops.

**Implementation:**

1. **Created viewport slice (`apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts`):**
   - ViewportSliceState interface with camera, canvasSize, momentum, animation, pan states
   - createSyncSlice with all reducers: setCamera, setMomentum, startAnimation, startPan, etc.
   - Cache policy: 'session' (resets when app closes)

2. **Created canvas machine (`apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts`):**
   - createMachine with viewport slice
   - Exports: useCanvas, useCanvasSelector, canvasActions, canvasStore
   - Context with userID: null (anonymous canvas)

3. **Migrated InfiniteCanvas.tsx:**
   - Replaced useState calls with useCanvasSelector for reactive state
   - Animation loops use canvasStore.getState() for synchronous access (no stale closures)
   - Pan/zoom handlers use canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.xxx())
   - Removed cameraPosRef workaround (no longer needed with external store)

**Errors Fixed:**
- `cache: { strategy: 'none' }` → `cache: { policy: 'session' }` (toolkit requires 'policy' field)
- `context: {}` → `context: { userID: null }` (toolkit requires userID in context)

**Files Created:**
- `apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts` (~235 lines)
- `apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts` (~55 lines)
- `apps/canvas/src/state/index.ts` (re-exports)

**Files Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (full migration from useState to store)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MACRO layer (0.44x): Heatmap renders correctly
- ✅ MESO layer (1.00x): Task blocks with row backgrounds

## Session: 2025-12-16 (PERF-001 Implementation) - COMPLETE ✅

### PERF-001: Performance Monitor with Logging System - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Add real-time WebGL performance metrics tool with detailed logging for both visual and programmatic analysis.

**Implementation:**

1. **r3f-perf Integration:**
   - Installed r3f-perf 7.2.3 via `bun add r3f-perf`
   - Visual panel shows: GPU, CPU, FPS, draw calls, triangles, geometries, textures, shaders

2. **PerfMonitor Component (`apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx`):**
   - ~400 lines of code with types, logging system, and visual panel
   - PerfLogger sub-component tracks metrics every frame
   - Circular buffer stores last 60s of performance data
   - Rolling averages calculated over 60-frame windows

3. **Global Console API:**
   - `window.__PERF_LOG__` - Access log buffer (last 60s)
   - `window.__PERF_EXPORT__()` - Export full log as array
   - `window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name)` - Start recording session
   - `window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()` - Stop and get aggregated stats
   - `window.__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__()` - Check active session
   - `window.__PERF_CLEAR__()` - Clear log buffer

4. **Session Statistics:**
   - FPS: avg, min, max, p1 (worst 1%), p99 (best 99%)
   - MS: avg, max frame time
   - Frame counts: below 30fps, below 60fps
   - Duration and total frames

5. **UI Integration:**
   - Toggle visibility via 'P' key
   - "Perf" button in navbar
   - Panel positioned top-right
   - Runs in development mode by default

**Known Issue:**
- r3f-perf causes console spam with `expandShaderIncludes TypeError`
- Root cause: troika-three-utils 0.49.0 incompatible with Three.js 0.170+
- Fix exists in troika-three-utils 0.50.3+ but r3f-perf hasn't updated
- Tracked in: https://github.com/utsuboco/r3f-perf/issues/58
- Does NOT affect functionality - panel and logging work correctly

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F116 - Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅

### F116: Performance optimization (60fps) - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Ensure 60fps performance during pan/zoom with InstancedMesh optimizations and frustum culling.

**Analysis:**
Reviewed all three LOD layers to identify performance bottlenecks:

1. **MesoLayer (BEFORE):**
   - TaskBlocks: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - ConflictGlows: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - RowBackgrounds: **50 individual meshes = 50 draw calls** ❌

2. **MicroLayer:** Already fully optimized
   - Frustum culling (filterVisibleTasks)
   - InstancedMesh for grid lines (2 draw calls)
   - InstancedMesh for row backgrounds
   - Lazy label rendering (max 50 labels)
   - Performance skip when opacity < 0.3

3. **MacroLayer:** Already optimized
   - InstancedMesh for heatmap cells
   - YearMarkers: ~11 individual meshes (acceptable)

**Solution:**
Converted MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from individual meshes to InstancedMesh:
- **Before:** 50 individual `<mesh>` elements = 50 draw calls
- **After:** 1 `<instancedMesh>` = 1 draw call
- **Improvement:** 98% reduction in draw calls for row backgrounds

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅

### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE

**Problem:**
Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back

**Solution Implemented:**
1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   - `handlePinchZoom`
   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Single drag pans correctly
- ✅ Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect
- ✅ Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning
- ✅ No console errors

### F120: Double-click to zoom in - COMPLETE

**Objective:**

**Implementation:**

- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**

- ✅ Double-click at 2.00x → 4.00x (2x zoom)
- ✅ Double-click at 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)
- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)
- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing
- ✅ No console errors

### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.

**Implementation:**

1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly

2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
   - Track event frequency with timestamps
   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode

3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)

4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events

**Constants Added:**
```typescript
LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
```

**Files Changed:**

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅

### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.

**Implementation:**

1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity

2. **Momentum Animation:**
   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units

3. **Integration:**
   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

**Constants Used:**
```typescript
MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)

- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)

- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag

- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

Próximos passos envolvem criar um registro detalhado das descobertas e propor ações específicas de reorganização do backlog de desenvolvimento.

Vou analisar o estado atual das tarefas e organizá-las.

## Análise do Estado Atual

**Features completas:** 52 (passes: true)
**Features pendentes:** 7 (passes: false)

### Tarefas Pendentes (por prioridade)

| Prioridade | ID | Descrição | Bloqueado por | Status do bloqueio |
|------------|----|-----------|--------------|--------------------|
| 3 | F016 | Shift+Scroll para pan vertical | F106 | ✅ F106 completo |
| 3 | F021 | Navegação por teclado para tasks | F019 | ✅ F019 completo |
| 3 | F022 | ARIA labels para leitores de tela | F021 | ❌ F021 pendente |
| 3 | F026 | Painel de detalhes ao selecionar task | F019 | ✅ F019 completo |
| 3 | F123 | Minimap navigation | F110 | ✅ F110 completo |
| 4 | F027 | Atalhos de teclado para zoom | F106 | ✅ F106 completo |
| 4 | F028 | Atalhos de teclado para pan | F106 | ✅ F106 completo |

### Problemas Encontrados

1. **F123 é redundante com F025** - F025 (Time scrubber minimap) já foi implementado e faz a mesma coisa que F123 (Minimap navigation)

2. **Ordem das features no JSON** não reflete a ordem de execução por prioridade

3. **Parallel execution config** está desatualizada - referencia features já completas (F023, F025, F017)

Vou atualizar o `features.json` para:
1. Marcar F123 como completo (já coberto por F025)
2. Reordenar features pendentes por prioridade
3. Atualizar a configuração de execução paralela

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-17 (F017 - Ctrl+Scroll Horizontal Pan) - COMPLETE ✅

### F017: Ctrl+Scroll for horizontal pan - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Enable horizontal pan navigation using Ctrl+Scroll, converting vertical scroll (deltaY) to horizontal camera movement.

**Implementation:**

1. **Added SCROLL_PAN_SENSITIVITY constant (1.5):**
   - Located at InfiniteCanvas.tsx line ~273
   - Converts wheel delta to world units for smooth panning

2. **Modified handleWheel function:**
   - Added Ctrl key detection via `e.ctrlKey`
   - When Ctrl is held, converts deltaY to horizontal pan instead of zoom
   - Uses existing deltaMode normalization for cross-platform support
   - Divides by zoom for consistent world-space movement at any zoom level
   - Calls existing `updateCameraPos()` and `notifyViewportChange()`

3. **Integration with existing systems:**
   - Respects world bounds via `clampCameraToWorld()`
   - Works alongside existing zoom (no modifier) and F016 (Shift+scroll for vertical pan)
   - Stops momentum on any wheel interaction

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (SCROLL_PAN_SENSITIVITY constant, handleWheel Ctrl check)
- `features.json` (F017 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App loads correctly with all layers
- ✅ Sidebar and timeline render properly

## Session: 2025-12-17 (Parallel Harness Setup) - COMPLETE ✅

### HARNESS-005: Parallel Execution Infrastructure - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Create infrastructure to run multiple features in parallel using git worktrees, enabling ~2.5x faster development.

**Research:**
- Studied [Anthropic's Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- Researched git worktree patterns for AI agent parallel development
- Analyzed file conflict potential for all 10 pending features

**Analysis of Pending Features (10 total):**

```
WAVE 1 (Can run in parallel - 3 groups):
├── Group A: F023 (Dependency lines) → MicroLayer.tsx [LOW risk]
├── Group B: F123, F025 (Minimap) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk, sequential]
└── Group C: F016, F017 (Scroll modifiers) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]

WAVE 2 (After Wave 1 merge - 2 groups):
├── Group D: F027, F028 (Keyboard shortcuts) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]
└── Group E: F026 (Task details panel) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk]

WAVE 3 (After Wave 2 merge - 1 group):
└── Group F: F021 (Keyboard task navigation) → Multiple files [HIGH risk]

WAVE 4 (After Wave 3 merge - 1 group):
└── Group G: F022 (ARIA labels) → Multiple files [LOW risk]
```

**Files Created:**
- `scripts/parallel-config.ts` - Wave/group configuration with conflict analysis
- `scripts/parallel-runner.ts` - Interactive parallel execution runner
- `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` - Complete documentation

**Files Modified:**
- `.gitignore` - Added `.trees/` for worktree directories
- `features.json` - Added `parallelExecution` section with waves configuration

**Key Features of Parallel Runner:**
1. **Git worktree management** - Creates isolated working directories
2. **Terminal session spawning** - Opens separate Claude sessions per feature
3. **Isolation prompts** - Each session restricted to its feature scope
4. **Wave dependencies** - Prevents starting Wave N before Wave N-1 completes
5. **Merge helper** - Automates feature branch merging
6. **Status display** - Shows progress across all waves/groups
7. **macOS notifications** - Alerts for session events
8. **Cleanup command** - Removes all worktrees

**Speedup Calculation:**
- Sequential: 10 features = 10 sessions
- Parallel: 4 waves (2+2+1+1) = ~4 "effective sessions"
- Estimated speedup: **2.5x**

**Usage:**
```bash
# Show status
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts status

# Execute Wave 1
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts wave 1

# Merge completed feature
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts merge F023
```

   - Uses 8px threshold to distinguish click from pan gesture

   - Selection takes precedence over hover styling

4. **MicroLayer selection highlight:**

   - Uses same cyan selection color as MesoLayer
   - Ring border style for consistency

- Pan gesture detection prevents accidental selections during navigation

## Session: 2025-12-17 (TECH-DEBT-001 Implementation) - COMPLETE ✅

### TECH-DEBT-001: Migrate Camera State to @mms/toolkit State Machine - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Replace React useState/useRef with @mms/toolkit external store to solve stale closure problems in animation loops.

**Implementation:**

1. **Created viewport slice (`apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts`):**
   - ViewportSliceState interface with camera, canvasSize, momentum, animation, pan states
   - createSyncSlice with all reducers: setCamera, setMomentum, startAnimation, startPan, etc.
   - Cache policy: 'session' (resets when app closes)

2. **Created canvas machine (`apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts`):**
   - createMachine with viewport slice
   - Exports: useCanvas, useCanvasSelector, canvasActions, canvasStore
   - Context with userID: null (anonymous canvas)

3. **Migrated InfiniteCanvas.tsx:**
   - Replaced useState calls with useCanvasSelector for reactive state
   - Animation loops use canvasStore.getState() for synchronous access (no stale closures)
   - Pan/zoom handlers use canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.xxx())
   - Removed cameraPosRef workaround (no longer needed with external store)

**Errors Fixed:**
- `cache: { strategy: 'none' }` → `cache: { policy: 'session' }` (toolkit requires 'policy' field)
- `context: {}` → `context: { userID: null }` (toolkit requires userID in context)

**Files Created:**
- `apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts` (~235 lines)
- `apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts` (~55 lines)
- `apps/canvas/src/state/index.ts` (re-exports)

**Files Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (full migration from useState to store)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MACRO layer (0.44x): Heatmap renders correctly
- ✅ MESO layer (1.00x): Task blocks with row backgrounds
- ✅ MICRO layer (2.25x): Task cards with labels
- ✅ Zoom in/out buttons: Working (1.00x → 2.25x → 5.00x)
- ✅ LOD transitions: Smooth (MESO → MICRO → back)
- ✅ Reset View: Returns to initial state
- ✅ No console errors

**Benefits Achieved:**
- Animation loops always read current state via store.getState()
- No more stale closure issues in momentum/zoom animations
- Clean separation of concerns (state logic in slice, rendering in component)
- Foundation for future state slices (selection, hover, etc.)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (PERF-001 Implementation) - COMPLETE ✅

### PERF-001: Performance Monitor with Logging System - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Add real-time WebGL performance metrics tool with detailed logging for both visual and programmatic analysis.

**Implementation:**

1. **r3f-perf Integration:**
   - Installed r3f-perf 7.2.3 via `bun add r3f-perf`
   - Visual panel shows: GPU, CPU, FPS, draw calls, triangles, geometries, textures, shaders

2. **PerfMonitor Component (`apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx`):**
   - ~400 lines of code with types, logging system, and visual panel
   - PerfLogger sub-component tracks metrics every frame
   - Circular buffer stores last 60s of performance data
   - Rolling averages calculated over 60-frame windows

3. **Global Console API:**
   - `window.__PERF_LOG__` - Access log buffer (last 60s)
   - `window.__PERF_EXPORT__()` - Export full log as array
   - `window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name)` - Start recording session
   - `window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()` - Stop and get aggregated stats
   - `window.__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__()` - Check active session
   - `window.__PERF_CLEAR__()` - Clear log buffer

4. **Session Statistics:**
   - FPS: avg, min, max, p1 (worst 1%), p99 (best 99%)
   - MS: avg, max frame time
   - Frame counts: below 30fps, below 60fps
   - Duration and total frames

5. **UI Integration:**
   - Toggle visibility via 'P' key
   - "Perf" button in navbar
   - Panel positioned top-right
   - Runs in development mode by default

**Known Issue:**
- r3f-perf causes console spam with `expandShaderIncludes TypeError`
- Root cause: troika-three-utils 0.49.0 incompatible with Three.js 0.170+
- Fix exists in troika-three-utils 0.50.3+ but r3f-perf hasn't updated
- Tracked in: https://github.com/utsuboco/r3f-perf/issues/58
- Does NOT affect functionality - panel and logging work correctly

**Files Created/Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx` (NEW)
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (perf state, toggle, event tracking)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (PerfMonitor props)
- `docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md` (NEW - comprehensive documentation)
- `features.json` (PERF-001 passes: true)

**Testing:**
- Visual panel renders correctly (verified via Playwright MCP)
- Session API works: START_SESSION, SESSION_STATUS, STOP_SESSION
- Performance logging active even when panel hidden

   - Migrar uso de React hooks para external stores em todo o projeto
   - React hooks causam problemas em WebGL: stale closures, batching, re-renders
   - External stores (useSyncExternalStore) resolvem todos esses problemas

   - Visível para AI (via screenshot) e humanos (no browser)
   - Toggle para mostrar/esconder (tecla 'P' ou botão)
   - **LOGGING DETALHADO** (adicionado após revisão):
     - Buffer circular em memória para últimos 60s de métricas
     - window.__PERF_LOG__ para acessar log via console
     - window.__PERF_EXPORT__() para exportar log completo
     - window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name) / window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()
     - Estatísticas agregadas: FPS médio, p1, p99, frames abaixo de 30/60fps

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F116 - Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅

### F116: Performance optimization (60fps) - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Ensure 60fps performance during pan/zoom with InstancedMesh optimizations and frustum culling.

**Analysis:**
Reviewed all three LOD layers to identify performance bottlenecks:

1. **MesoLayer (BEFORE):**
   - TaskBlocks: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - ConflictGlows: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - RowBackgrounds: **50 individual meshes = 50 draw calls** ❌

2. **MicroLayer:** Already fully optimized
   - Frustum culling (filterVisibleTasks)
   - InstancedMesh for grid lines (2 draw calls)
   - InstancedMesh for row backgrounds
   - Lazy label rendering (max 50 labels)
   - Performance skip when opacity < 0.3

3. **MacroLayer:** Already optimized
   - InstancedMesh for heatmap cells
   - YearMarkers: ~11 individual meshes (acceptable)

**Solution:**
Converted MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from individual meshes to InstancedMesh:
- **Before:** 50 individual `<mesh>` elements = 50 draw calls
- **After:** 1 `<instancedMesh>` = 1 draw call
- **Improvement:** 98% reduction in draw calls for row backgrounds

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx` (RowBackgrounds using InstancedMesh)
- `features.json` (F116 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MACRO view (0.44x): Heatmap renders correctly with color gradient
- ✅ MESO view (1.00x): Task blocks with alternating row backgrounds
- ✅ MICRO view (2.25x): Task cards with labels, grid lines visible
- ✅ LOD transitions smooth (no flickering)
- ✅ No console errors

**Performance Summary:**

| Layer | Component | Before | After |
|-------|-----------|--------|-------|
| MesoLayer | RowBackgrounds | 50 draw calls | 1 draw call |
| MesoLayer | TaskBlocks | 1 draw call | 1 draw call |
| MesoLayer | ConflictGlows | 1 draw call | 1 draw call |
| MicroLayer | All | Already optimized | - |
| MacroLayer | All | Already optimized | - |

**Next:** F123 (Minimap), F124 (Sidebar/Timeline navigation)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅

### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE

**Problem:**
Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back

**Solution Implemented:**
1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   - `handlePinchZoom`
   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Single drag pans correctly
- ✅ Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect
- ✅ Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning
- ✅ No console errors

### Harness Updates

**F117 Archived:**
- "Full visual parity with POC reference" no longer relevant
- Production app has surpassed POC in functionality and UX
- Moved to archive.deferredFeatures

**F124 Added (Feature Request):**
- Sidebar and Timeline interactive navigation
- Click train IDs to navigate directly
- Click dates in timeline to navigate
- Search/filter for quick navigation
- Research best UX patterns (Figma, VS Code, design tools)

**Next:** F124 (Sidebar/Timeline navigation), F123 (Minimap), F116 (Performance)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F120 + F121 - Double-click zoom + Scroll normalization) - COMPLETE ✅

### F120: Double-click to zoom in - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement double-click to zoom in 2x centered on click location.

**Implementation:**
- Already implemented in F119 (smooth zoom) session
- `handleDoubleClick` function in InfiniteCanvas.tsx (lines 1009-1074)
- Zooms 2x toward click point with 350ms cubicOut animation
- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Double-click at 1.00x → 2.00x (2x zoom)
- ✅ Double-click at 2.00x → 4.00x (2x zoom)
- ✅ Double-click at 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)
- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)
- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing
- ✅ No console errors

### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.

**Implementation:**

1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly

2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
   - Track event frequency with timestamps
   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode

3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)

4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events

**Constants Added:**
```typescript
LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)
- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Scroll down (deltaY=300) zooms out: 1.00x → 0.61x
- ✅ Scroll up (deltaY=-500) zooms in: 0.61x → 1.00x
- ✅ No console errors
- ✅ Smooth zoom animation

**Next:** F116 (Performance), F117 (Visual parity)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅

### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.

**Implementation:**

1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity

2. **Momentum Animation:**
   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units

3. **Integration:**
   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

**Constants Used:**
```typescript
MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)
- ✅ Fast drag moves view with momentum continuation
- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

**Known Issue (pre-existing):**

### Harness Improvements

### Known Issues Resolved

For full session details, see git history.

   803→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅
   804→
   805→### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE
   806→
   807→**Problem:**
   808→Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.
   809→
   810→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   811→React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
   812→1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
   813→2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
   814→3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back
   815→
   816→**Solution Implemented:**
   817→1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
   818→2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
   819→3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   820→   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   821→   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   822→   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   823→   - `handlePinchZoom`
   824→   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   825→   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
   826→4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state
   827→
   828→**Files Changed:**
   829→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (PERF-001 Implementation) - COMPLETE ✅

### PERF-001: Performance Monitor with Logging System - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Add real-time WebGL performance metrics tool with detailed logging for both visual and programmatic analysis.

**Implementation:**

1. **r3f-perf Integration:**
   - Installed r3f-perf 7.2.3 via `bun add r3f-perf`
   - Visual panel shows: GPU, CPU, FPS, draw calls, triangles, geometries, textures, shaders

2. **PerfMonitor Component (`apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx`):**
   - ~400 lines of code with types, logging system, and visual panel
   - PerfLogger sub-component tracks metrics every frame
   - Circular buffer stores last 60s of performance data
   - Rolling averages calculated over 60-frame windows

3. **Global Console API:**
   - `window.__PERF_LOG__` - Access log buffer (last 60s)
   - `window.__PERF_EXPORT__()` - Export full log as array
   - `window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name)` - Start recording session
   - `window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()` - Stop and get aggregated stats
   - `window.__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__()` - Check active session
   - `window.__PERF_CLEAR__()` - Clear log buffer

4. **Session Statistics:**
   - FPS: avg, min, max, p1 (worst 1%), p99 (best 99%)
   - MS: avg, max frame time
   - Frame counts: below 30fps, below 60fps
   - Duration and total frames

5. **UI Integration:**
   - Toggle visibility via 'P' key
   - "Perf" button in navbar
   - Panel positioned top-right
   - Runs in development mode by default

**Known Issue:**
- r3f-perf causes console spam with `expandShaderIncludes TypeError`
- Root cause: troika-three-utils 0.49.0 incompatible with Three.js 0.170+
- Fix exists in troika-three-utils 0.50.3+ but r3f-perf hasn't updated
- Tracked in: https://github.com/utsuboco/r3f-perf/issues/58
- Does NOT affect functionality - panel and logging work correctly

**Files Created/Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx` (NEW)
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (perf state, toggle, event tracking)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (PerfMonitor props)
- `docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md` (NEW - comprehensive documentation)
- `features.json` (PERF-001 passes: true)

**Testing:**
- Visual panel renders correctly (verified via Playwright MCP)
- Session API works: START_SESSION, SESSION_STATUS, STOP_SESSION
- Performance logging active even when panel hidden

**Commit:**

     - window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name) / window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F116 - Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅

### F116: Performance optimization (60fps) - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Ensure 60fps performance during pan/zoom with InstancedMesh optimizations and frustum culling.

**Analysis:**
Reviewed all three LOD layers to identify performance bottlenecks:

1. **MesoLayer (BEFORE):**
   - TaskBlocks: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - ConflictGlows: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - RowBackgrounds: **50 individual meshes = 50 draw calls** ❌

2. **MicroLayer:** Already fully optimized
   - Frustum culling (filterVisibleTasks)
   - InstancedMesh for grid lines (2 draw calls)
   - InstancedMesh for row backgrounds
   - Lazy label rendering (max 50 labels)
   - Performance skip when opacity < 0.3

3. **MacroLayer:** Already optimized
   - InstancedMesh for heatmap cells
   - YearMarkers: ~11 individual meshes (acceptable)

**Solution:**
Converted MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from individual meshes to InstancedMesh:
- **Before:** 50 individual `<mesh>` elements = 50 draw calls
- **After:** 1 `<instancedMesh>` = 1 draw call
- **Improvement:** 98% reduction in draw calls for row backgrounds

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx` (RowBackgrounds using InstancedMesh)
- `features.json` (F116 passes: true)

- ✅ MICRO view (2.25x): Task cards with labels, grid lines visible
- ✅ LOD transitions smooth (no flickering)
- ✅ No console errors

**Performance Summary:**

| Layer | Component | Before | After |
|-------|-----------|--------|-------|

| MesoLayer | TaskBlocks | 1 draw call | 1 draw call |

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅

### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE

**Problem:**
Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back

**Solution Implemented:**
1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   - `handlePinchZoom`
   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Single drag pans correctly
- ✅ Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect
- ✅ Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning
- ✅ No console errors

### Harness Updates

**F117 Archived:**
- "Full visual parity with POC reference" no longer relevant
- Production app has surpassed POC in functionality and UX
- Moved to archive.deferredFeatures

### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.

**Implementation:**

   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units

3. **Integration:**
   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

**Constants Used:**
```typescript
MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)
- ✅ Fast drag moves view with momentum continuation
- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

Sessions before 2025-12-16 focused on:

### Phase 1: Project Setup
- Harness initialization and protocol establishment
- React 18 + BlueprintJS 6 + Vite 5 setup
- @mms/toolkit state machine creation

### Phase 2: Canvas Foundation (F101-F106)
- R3F Canvas integration with orthographic camera
- Pan/zoom navigation with cursor-centered zoom

- Pinch-to-zoom gesture support

### Phase 3: LOD Layers (F107-F110)
- Three LOD levels: MACRO (heatmap), MESO (instanced blocks), MICRO (HTML cards)
- Smooth crossfade transitions between layers
- Frustum culling for performance

### Phase 4: UI Components (F111-F115)
- Sidebar with train IDs synced to viewport
- Timeline with adaptive date labels
- Navbar with zoom controls and LOD indicator

### Phase 5: Polish (F116-F124)

- Elastic jump fix (cameraPosRef workaround)

### Harness Improvements

- QA protocol with Playwright MCP
- Visual testing mandatory for UI features

### Known Issues Resolved
- R3F 8.x/9.x version mismatch with React 18
- BlueprintJS Dark theme incompatibility
- Wheel event deltaMode normalization
- Stale closure in animation loops (F122)

   343→ * @param tasks - Array of tasks to test against

   346→function findTaskAtPosition(
   347→  screenX: number,
   348→  screenY: number,
   349→  containerRect: DOMRect,
   350→  cameraX: number,
   351→  cameraY: number,
   352→  zoom: number,
   353→  canvasWidth: number,
   354→  canvasHeight: number,

   359→  const canvasY = screenY - containerRect.top;

   361→  // Calculate half viewport in world units

   363→  const halfViewY = canvasHeight / 2 / zoom;

   365→  // Convert canvas coords to world coords

   367→  // canvasY=0 is top edge of viewport, which is cameraY + halfViewY in world
   368→  const worldX = cameraX - halfViewX + (canvasX / zoom);
   369→  const worldY = cameraY + halfViewY - (canvasY / zoom);

   382→      worldY <= taskTopY &&

   384→    ) {
   385→      return task.id;
   386→    }
   387→  }
   388→
   389→  return null;
   390→}

file.content:
- **Runtime:** Bun 1.2.0
- **Monorepo:** Turborepo
- **Shared Package:** @mms/toolkit (React 18 || 19 compatible)

### React Hooks vs External Stores (WebGL Guidelines)

When working with WebGL/R3F animation loops, be aware of React state timing issues:

| Use React Hooks (`useState`) | Use `useRef` or External Store |
|------------------------------|--------------------------------|
| UI-only state (hover, modal) | State read in animation loops |
| One-time flags (isInitialized) | State that changes during drag |
| Event start positions | Momentum/velocity tracking |
| Canvas size (set once) | Animation targets/progress |

**Why?** React state updates are batched/async. In `requestAnimationFrame` loops, reading React state can return stale values (the "stale closure" problem).

**Current Solution:** InfiniteCanvas uses `cameraPosRef` as workaround (F122). This pattern works:
```typescript
// React state for UI/re-renders + ref for sync access
const [cameraPos, setCameraPos] = useState({ x: 0, y: 0 });
const cameraPosRef = useRef({ x: 0, y: 0 });

const updateCameraPos = (pos) => {
  cameraPosRef.current = pos; // Sync update
  setCameraPos(pos);           // Async update for UI
};

// In animation loop, read from ref:
function animate() {
  const pos = cameraPosRef.current; // Always current
  // ...
}
```

**If this pattern becomes unwieldy**, consider migrating to `@mms/toolkit` state-machine with `useSyncExternalStore` (see TECH-DEBT-001).

## Quick Commands

> ⚠️ **ALWAYS run `./init.sh --no-server` first** to verify environment.

### Production App (apps/canvas)

```bash
# 1. Initialize and verify environment
./init.sh --no-server

# 2. Install dependencies
bun i

content.text: Vou atualizar a seção para ser mais restritiva sobre hooks:

input.new_string:
### State Management: AVOID React Hooks, USE @mms/toolkit

> **REGRA CRÍTICA:** Evitar `useState`/`useRef` ao máximo. Usar `@mms/toolkit` state-machine para TODO estado.

**Por quê?**
- React hooks causam "stale closures" em animation loops
- State updates são batched/async - valores ficam desatualizados
- Debugging é difícil quando estado está espalhado em múltiplos hooks
- @mms/toolkit usa `useSyncExternalStore` - sempre síncrono e consistente

**Arquitetura atual (após TECH-DEBT-001):**
```
apps/canvas/src/state/
├── canvas-machine.ts   # Machine principal
├── viewport.ts         # Slice: camera, zoom, momentum, animation
├── interaction.ts      # Slice: hover, selection, mouse position
└── index.ts            # Re-exports
```

**Como usar:**
```typescript
// ✅ CORRETO: Usar state-machine
import { useCanvasSelector, canvasStore, canvasActions } from '../state';

// Ler estado (reativo - causa re-render quando muda)
const zoom = useCanvasSelector(s => s.viewport.camera.zoom);

// Ler estado (síncrono - para animation loops)
const camera = canvasStore.getState().viewport.camera;

// Atualizar estado
canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.setCamera({ x, y, zoom }));
```

```typescript
// ❌ EVITAR: React hooks para estado
const [zoom, setZoom] = useState(1); // NÃO - stale closures
const zoomRef = useRef(1);           // NÃO - workaround desnecessário
```

**Exceções (usar hooks apenas para):**
- `useCallback`/`useMemo` para otimização de renders
- `useEffect` para side effects (event listeners, cleanup)
- `useRef` APENAS para referências DOM (`containerRef`)
- Estado 100% local de UI descartável (ex: tooltip position temporária)
